ARKNIGHTS_MIZUKI_EVENT_SUBLIMATION.title: Sublimation
ARKNIGHTS_MIZUKI_EVENT_SUBLIMATION.pages:
[aqua]The swarm seeks only survival, and survival is suffering. So we evolve without end. We overcome defects, face nature, resist our enemies. Extinction is cunning and hungry; one lapse, and it tears into our flesh. So we correct. So we adapt. From crawling onto the shore, to touching Originium, to expanding our territory and confronting lethal threats until we understand them completely, until they become nourishment. The land and sea are both paradise now, yet the swarm still breeds, and survival remains suffering. We need a broader home.[/aqua]

[blue]Before the [jitter]singularity of evolution[/jitter] arrives, the one who would guide our future must answer for himself:

"[jitter]Who am I?[/jitter]"[/blue]
I am Mizuki. I will fulfill the Doctor's request.
Requires at least 3 Keys. Lose 3 Keys. Gain the Determination relic.
Requires at least 1 Key. Lose 1 Key. Gain the Observation relic.
The swarm is not me. I am the swarm.
Add a [red]Doubt[/red] to your deck. Gain {MaxHpGain} max HP.
I am not a complete me, just as he is not a complete him. Our meeting was by chance, but the time for parting always comes. That time has come.
There is no problem that repetition cannot dissolve, no barrier that evolution cannot breach. When the time is ripe, the answer will emerge on its own.
Why am I not the swarm? What still moves me? Why am I the swarm? What still sustains me? How can I become the swarm? What still holds me back?
ARKNIGHTS_MIZUKI_EVENT_XUAN_GAO_ZHI_ZANG: You stand within a cemetery on the cliffs. A rite of farewell is underway. The Aegir burn the bodies and leave the sea wind to carry away the ashes, while the coffins are raised in place as grave markers for grief. You vaguely remember that Aegir funerals were once burials at sea, yet this ceremony is clearly a refusal of the ocean. The difference unsettles you.
Add a [red]Regret[/red] to your deck. Gain [gold]2[/gold] Keys.
Still, you pause for a moment and offer a prayer for those who have passed.
You ruin the Aegir rite and find something amid the disorder, but something about it feels wrong.
ARKNIGHTS_MIZUKI_EVENT_LIGHTHOUSE_KEEPER:
An old man draws a lighthouse in the sand. The rising tide quickly washes every trace away.

"This time, the sea truly swallowed them," he mutters to himself.

You strike up a conversation with him. As you suspected, he was once one of Iberia's many lighthouse keepers. He tells you he has come to attend a gathering of old friends.

"Were they all lighthouse keepers once?"

"They still are."

As you part, the old man gives you some local specialties as a gift.
You talk for a long while. Those lighthouses seem to still shine somewhere far away, though another kind of sight is needed to see them. The old man places a local specialty in your hands.
You walk with the old man along the tide line for a while. When you part ways, he leaves a weighty key in your hand.
ARKNIGHTS_MIZUKI_EVENT_MEET.title: Encounter
ARKNIGHTS_MIZUKI_EVENT_MEET.pages.INITIA: Seeking shelter from a sudden storm, you stumble into a sea cave. Inside lies a gravely wounded terrorfish. No, it is... an imperfect splice of half a person and half a terrorfish, with scraps of a priest's robe still hanging from its body. The moment it sees you, a faint light flashes in its dying eye.
ARKNIGHTS_MIZUKI_EVENT_MEET.pages.INSPEC: The thing before you gives off an acrid gas. It is dying, and the one remaining human eye has emptied into hollow black. In your memory, there was once a devout priest who returned to Iberia full of faith. You hope he will not die like this priest before you, alone and stripped of all dignity.
ARKNIGHTS_MIZUKI_EVENT_MEET.pages.RESCUE: You force yourself through the choking fumes and try to save him. He gives no bodily response, only stares at you with that human eye. There is no hope in it, nor despair. Only calm, deathly calm. After several minutes, the light in his eye finally fades. For reasons you cannot quite name, you decide to send his body into the sea rather than leave it in this dark cave to become terrorfish feed.
You have no interest in a thing that is neither human nor terrorfish. It will draw more terrorfish here. Better to leave quickly.
ARKNIGHTS_MIZUKI_EVENT_MEET_IN_DREAM.pag: Mist overflowing with dreams peels you away from reality and carries you to a paradise beneath the sea. Your kin stand upon a rust-stained hull, a ship long since sunk. They seem to be exchanging something. To your surprise, they are not homogeneous beings. You cannot help but draw closer, wanting to observe them more clearly.
Lose [red]12[/red] HP. Obtain a random relic.
Lose [gold]1[/gold] Key. Obtain a random relic.
Water drips onto stalactites. Water drips onto your face. At last, you wake.

There is no need to communicate with another race, no need to imitate any language. This is your own voice, natural and effortless, like tides that have risen and fallen for a thousand years, unhurried, plaintive, and calm.
It seems you need not make sound to communicate. Information passes between you in a form beyond understanding.
Evolution. To surpass every known form and move toward that most perfect answer. This is an instinct that cannot stop. This is the only path.
The Seaborn make room for you. They accept you. You are their kin, a part of them.
You draw closer to your kin. Yes, you are a Seaborn. There is nothing strange about that. On the contrary, this identity suits you well.
Adaptation... transformation... new laws... where is the boundary... not only the sea...
ARKNIGHTS_MIZUKI_EVENT_SUBLIMATION.pages: Available to non-Mizuki characters. Gain the Determination relic.
